name: Veracode Application Security API
what_it_does: Veracode REST API for application security platform covering SAST (binary analysis), DAST, SCA, and manual pen testing. Enables AI agents to query application scan results, retrieve vulnerability findings with remediation guidance, manage policies, and integrate AppSec data into DevSecOps workflows. Veracode is notable for its binary analysis approach (no source code required).
use_cases: Querying application scan results and policy compliance from AppSec pipeline agents
Retrieving vulnerability findings with CWE mappings from DevSecOps automation agents
Managing scan submissions and pipeline gate decisions from CI/CD security agents
Integrating Veracode SCA results into dependency management from supply chain agents
not_for: Runtime application protection (Veracode is pre-production scanning)
best_when: An enterprise DevSecOps team using Veracode wants AI agents to automate scan lifecycle, surface policy violations, and feed AppSec intelligence into remediation tracking. AUTHORIZED USE ONLY.
avoid_when: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Vulnerability findings reveal exploitable code paths. Veracode's binary analysis is powerful but has higher latency — design for async scan workflows.

auth.notes: Veracode HMAC-based API credentials (API ID + API Key). HMAC signature required for all requests. Veracode Python SDK handles signing.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per application pricing; SAST, DAST, SCA modules
pricing.notes: Enterprise AppSec platform with per-application licensing. API access included with subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Veracode API: rate limits per account; binary scan analysis can take hours

known_agent_gotchas: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Vulnerability findings reveal exploitable code paths
No public MCP server — HMAC-signed REST API required
HMAC authentication is complex — use official Python SDK for easier integration
Binary scans are async and can take hours — design agents for long-polling
Veracode has legacy XML API and newer REST API — use REST API v2 only
agent_readiness.security_notes: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY. Application vulnerability data reveals exploitable code paths. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. HMAC credentials are sensitive — never log or expose.

security_score_components.security_notes: Application vulnerability data including source code paths. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. HMAC-signed credentials.
